edge_hypothesis: EMA crossover currently produces PF=1.03 with 389 trades (NOISE status). The diagnostic spec ec6t4w8m tests high R:R (7-10:1) but NO time caps. ea9p5k2m proved that a 5-bar time cap transforms a mediocre signal (ema_rsi_atr) into an ACCEPT (PF=1.288). The hypothesis: EMA_9/EMA_21 crossovers generate a brief directional impulse in the first 3-7 bars. After that, the signal decays into noise as price oscillates around the new EMA alignment. By applying aggressive time caps (3-7 bars) with extreme R:R (8-12:1), we extract only the explosive initial impulse and cut all drift trades. The time cap acts as a de facto 'momentum decay filter' — if the crossover hasn't produced a large move within 3-5 bars, it's a false signal and we exit flat rather than holding through reversal. This tests whether ema_crossover's NOISE status is caused by holding too long, not by poor signal quality. Regime assumption: RANGING (crossover impulses should work best when price is oscillating around EMA convergence zones). Invalidation: if PF < 0.95 with 50+ trades across all time cap variants, the crossover signal itself is too weak and the template should be removed.

variants.name: crossover_impulse_3bar_10to1
variants.description: Ultra-aggressive impulse extraction: 3-bar time cap forces exit within 3 bars of entry. Only trades that produce an explosive move within 3 hours (1h TF) or 12 hours (4h TF) survive. 10:1 R:R captures rare large impulses while the time cap cuts the 80%+ of crossovers that produce no follow-through. Expect very low win rate (~15%) but massive winners.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 on bar close.
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 on bar close.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Data required: ETH OHLCV on 1h and 4h
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (15.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Exit on max bars in trade (3 bars) — impulse decay filter.
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 3-bar cap may exit before TP on most trades, producing many small time-exit trades
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 15.0x ATR_14 from entry (10:1 R:R).
Time cap: exit at bar 3 if neither stop nor TP hit.

components.notes: ATR-based stop/TP only, no volatility gate on entry

variants.name: crossover_impulse_5bar_8to1
variants.description: Matched to ea9p5k2m's winning 5-bar time cap. Tests whether the 5-bar sweet spot that produced an ACCEPT on ema_rsi_atr also works for the simpler ema_crossover signal. 8:1 R:R is slightly less extreme, giving more room for TP hits. This is the 'control' variant — if this outperforms the 3-bar and 7-bar variants, the 5-bar cap is universally optimal across templates.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 on bar close.
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 on bar close.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Data required: ETH OHLCV on 1h and 4h
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Exit on max bars in trade (5 bars) — ea9p5k2m-matched time cap.
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 5-bar cap may still be too short for ema_crossover which has slower signal propagation than ema_rsi_atr
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry (8:1 R:R).
Time cap: exit at bar 5 if neither stop nor TP hit.

components.notes: ATR-based stop/TP, 5-bar time cap matched to ea9p5k2m

variants.name: crossover_impulse_7bar_12to1
variants.description: Maximum R:R variant (12:1) with slightly relaxed 7-bar time cap. Tests the extreme lottery approach: most trades exit via time cap for small gains/losses, but rare explosive crossover impulses hit the 18x ATR TP for massive wins. If ema_crossover contains even a small fraction of genuinely explosive crossovers, the 12:1 R:R captures disproportionate value from them.
entry_long: EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 on bar close.
entry_short: EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 on bar close.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Data required: ETH OHLCV on 1h and 4h
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (18.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Exit on max bars in trade (7 bars) — extended impulse window.
Hypothesis failure mode guard: 12:1 R:R may produce extremely low win rate (<10%), requiring 100+ trades to evaluate statistically
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 18.0x ATR_14 from entry (12:1 R:R).
Time cap: exit at bar 7 if neither stop nor TP hit.

components.notes: ATR-based stop/TP, 7-bar time cap, extreme 12:1 R:R
